claims.claim_text: The race for District Attorney (covered by KCRA 3, Sacramento area) is being contested for the first time in 20 years.

provisional.explanation: This is a specific local political claim about the Sacramento County DA race. While it's plausible that the incumbent DA (Anne Marie Schubert or a predecessor) ran unopposed for multiple cycles, I cannot confirm the precise 20-year figure from my training data.

claims.claim_text: The candidate (Monsanto, a DA candidate in the KCRA-covered race) has been in the area for 58 years.

provisional.explanation: This is a specific biographical claim about a local district attorney candidate's length of residence. Without access to the video content or reliable background on this particular candidate, I cannot confirm or refute the 58-year figure from trained knowledge alone.

claims.claim_text: The candidate (Monsanto, a DA candidate) has worked as a prosecutor for 24 years.
Todd Leras worked as a district attorney in the Sacramento-area DA race covered by KCRA 3.

markers.name: absolutism
markers.excerpt: I am the most qualified candidate
markers.explanation: The candidate uses an absolute superlative claim ('the most qualified') without supporting evidence, a common campaign rhetorical device asserting unmatched standing.

markers.name: glittering_generalities
markers.excerpt: we have done a tremendous job, the entire team of reaching the voters
markers.explanation: Vague, positively-charged language ('tremendous job') is used to evoke approval without specifying what was accomplished.

markers.name: appeal_to_authority
markers.excerpt: THE DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL SAYS SHE HAS WHAT IT TAKES TO BE THE TOP PROSECUTOR
markers.explanation: The reporter frames credibility via the candidate's title/position rather than substantive qualifications, leveraging authority as the basis for the claim.
